rulings.Case Title: ALFONSO PAGANO JR ET AL VS. AKEBONO BRAKE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 1, 2025 in Department 304, Line 3. Defendant Hitachi Astemo Americas, Inc's Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Defendant's Alternative Motion for Summary Adjudication is GRANTED. Opposition filed. Reply filed. A. Evidentiary Objections Plaintiffs' objection to the Declaration of Nathan Beskow at 3-12 is SUSTAINED on the grounds of lack of foundation and hearsay as to events and conditions prior to 2003. (LAOSD Asbestos Cases (2023) 87 Cal.App.5th 939, 947-951 [corporate representative who did not work at defendant until 1994 lacked personal knowledge regarding events prior to her employment]; Ramirez v. Avon Products (2023) 87 Cal.App.5th 939, 951 [a declaration by a corporate witness about activities before she started working at the corporation cannot be based on her personal knowledge and must be hearsay.]) In Mr. Beskow's declaration, none of the facts he attests to is within the time period of his personal knowledge. Plaintiffs' objection to the Exhibit 1, the Declaration of Nathan Beskow in Support of Defendant's reply is SUSTAINED. A moving party may not rely on additional evidence filed with its reply papers. (Jay v. Mahaffey (2013) 218 Cal.App.4th 1522, 1537-1538.) B. Motion for Summary Judgment Defendant fails to sustain its initial burden of showing Plaintiff Alfonso Pagano Jr. ("Plaintiff") does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain needed evidence of asbestos exposure and causation. (Aguilar v. Atl. Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855.) Defendant relies on Plaintiff's deposition testimony and discovery responses arguing they are factually devoid. The Court disagrees. For the reasons set below, the Court finds that Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. C. Motion for Summary Adjudication Plaintiffs do not oppose Defendant's motion for summary adjudication of their claim for punitive damages, which is GRANTED. *Complete tentative ruling has been electronically served to Counsel. The moving party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 304 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on April 1, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely via zoom: Meeting ID 160 757 8308; Passcode: 485029. Face coverings are optional. The Court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law and Motion department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: Their name, CSR and telephone number, and their individual work email address. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. = (EPS/304)

rulings.Case Title: ALFONSO PAGANO JR ET AL VS. AKEBONO BRAKE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 1, 2025 in Department 304, Line 3. Defendant Rockwell Automation's Motion for Summary Judgment, or in the Alternative, Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Opposition filed. Reply filed. A. Evidentiary Objections a. Plaintiffs Evidentiary Objections Plaintiff's objections to the Declaration of Andrew Jaap at 7-17 are SUSTAINED on the grounds of inadmissible hearsay and lack of foundation as to events and conditions prior to 2000. (LAOSD Asbestos Cases (2023) 87 Cal.App.5th 939, 947-951 [corporate representative who did not work with defendant until 1994 lacked personal knowledge regarding events prior to her employment]; Code Civ. Proc. 2025.620; Evid. Code. 1200, 1291(a), 1292(a); 401-403.) Moreover, neither Defendant nor Mr. Jaap have made any attempt to establish that Defendant's records qualify as business records under Evidence Code section 1271 or any other hearsay exception. (See id. at 952.) b. Defendant's Evidentiary Objections Defendant's objections are OVERRULED. B. Motion for Summary Judgment Defendant (successor to Allen-Bradley) fails to sustain its initial burden of showing Plaintiff Alfonso Pagano Jr. ("Plaintiff") does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain needed evidence of asbestos exposure and causation. (Aguilar v. Atl. Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855.) Defendant relies on Plaintiff's deposition testimony and discovery responses arguing they are factually devoid. The Court disagrees. For the reasons set below, the Court finds that Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. *Complete tentative ruling has been electronically served to Counsel. The moving party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 304 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on April 1, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely via zoom: Meeting ID 160 757 8308; Passcode: 485029. Face coverings are optional. The Court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law and Motion department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: Their name, CSR and telephone number, and their individual work email address. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. = (EPS/304)

rulings.Case Title: ALFONSO PAGANO JR ET AL VS. AKEBONO BRAKE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 1, 2025 in Department 304, Line 3. Defendant BW/IP, Inc and its Wholly-Owned Subsidiaries' Motion for Summary Judgment, or in the Alternative, Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Opposition filed. Reply filed. A. Evidentiary Objections a. Plaintiff's Evidentiary Objections Plaintiffs' objection to the Declaration of Frank Constanzo in its entirety is SUSTAINED on the grounds of inadmissible hearsay, lack of foundation, and lack of personal knowledge. (Evid. Code. 1200; 401-403, 702.) Mr. Constanzo lacks personal knowledge regarding Defendant's historical records, particularly for sales before he started working there in 1968. Mr. Constanzo's declaration must be based on personal knowledge, and he cannot testify to Defendant's record-keeping practices before his employment commenced. (LAOSD Asbestos Cases (2023) 87 Cal.App.5th 939, 947-951 [corporate representative who did not work with defendant until 1994 lacked personal knowledge regarding events prior to her employment].) Plaintiffs' evidentiary objection to Exhibit A to Frank Costanzo Declaration is SUSTAINED. Neither Defendant nor Mr. Constanzo has made any attempt to establish that Defendant's records qualify as business records under Evidence Code section 1271 or any other hearsay exception. (See id. at 952.) b. Defendant's Evidentiary Objections Defendant's objections to Deposition Transcript of Frank Constazo in its entirety is OVEERULED. While the reporter's certification page with the designated transcript pages is not included, the deposition transcript can be authenticated, and any omission is a curable defect. (Evid. Code 1400; People v. Skiles (2011) 51 Cal.4th 1178 [certification of an official writing under sections 1530 and 1531 establishes its reliability in the absence of evidence overcoming the presumption that it is a true and correct copy].) B. Motion for Summary Judgment Defendant fails to sustain its initial burden of showing Plaintiff Alfonso Pagano Jr. ("Plaintiff") does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain needed evidence of asbestos exposure and causation. (Aguilar v. Atl. Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855.) Defendant argues that it is impossible for Plaintiff to have worked with a Byron Jackson pump aboard the USS Kitty Hawk from 1969 to 1973. For the reasons set below, the Court finds that Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. *Complete tentative ruling has been electronically served to Counsel. The moving party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 304 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on April 1, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely via zoom: Meeting ID 160 757 8308; Passcode: 485029. Face coverings are optional. The Court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law and Motion department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: Their name, CSR and telephone number, and their individual work email address. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. = (EPS/304)

Defendant's Alternative Motion for Summary Adjudication is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Opposition filed. Reply filed. A. Evidentiary Objections Plaintiffs' objections to Defense Exhibits L, M, N, of the Deposition of Thomas Douglass taken in three separate matters is SUSTAINED on the grounds of lack of foundation and hearsay. B. Motion for Summary Judgment Defendant fails to sustain its initial burden of showing Plaintiff Alfonso Pagano Jr. ("Plaintiff") does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain needed evidence of asbestos exposure and causation. For the reasons set below, the Court finds that Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. C. Motion for Summary Adjudication Plaintiff does not oppose Defendant's motion for summary adjudication of the Third Cause of Action for Negligent Misrepresentation, Fourth Cause of Action for Fraud by Nondisclosure, and claim for punitive damages, which are GRANTED. rulings.Case Title: ALFONSO PAGANO JR ET AL VS. AKEBONO BRAKE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ; Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Of Plaintiffs Claim For Punitive Damages
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 1, 2025 in Department 304, Line 3. Defendant G. Denver and CO., LLC's (erroneously sued and served as "Gardner Denver, Inc.") Motion for Summary Judgment, or in the Alternative, Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Opposition filed. Reply filed. A. Judicial Notice Defendant's request for Judicial Notice of Select Pages from 1961 Navy Report of Final Acceptance Trials and Material Inspection of USS Kitty Hawk (CVA- 63), is GRANTED, with the caveat that the Court is not judicially noticing the truth of the allegations therein. (Evid. Code, 452, subds. (c), (d).) B. Evidentiary Objections a. Plaintiff's Evidentiary Objections Plaintiff's objections to Defense Exhibit E, the Declaration of Charles W. Jenkins (1961 Navy Report), is SUSTAINED as hearsay. (Evid. Code 1200, et seq.) Mr. Jenkins failed to authenticate this document. Moreover, Defendant did not include all the pages of this report. Lastly, while this document purports to be a 1961 Navy Report, Plaintiff did not serve aboard the USS Kitty Hawk until 1969. b. Defendant's Evidentiary Objections Defendant's objections in reply are OVERRULED. C. Motion for Summary Judgment Defendant fails to sustain its initial burden of showing Plaintiff Alfonso Pagano Jr. ("Plaintiff") does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain needed evidence of asbestos exposure and causation. (Aguilar v. Atl. Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855.) Defendant argues that Plaintiffs have no evidence that any asbestos component on any of its compressors removed by Plaintiff was supplied by Defendant. The Court disagrees. For the reasons set below, the Court finds that Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. D. Motion for Summary Adjudication Defendant's motion for summary adjudication as to the claim for punitive damages is DENIED. Defendant fails to meet its burden of demonstrating that Plaintiff will be unable to make a showing that Defendant acted with malice, fraud, or oppression. (Civ. Code 3294, subd. (a).) *Complete tentative ruling has been electronically served to Counsel. The moving party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 304 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on April 1, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely via zoom: Meeting ID 160 757 8308; Passcode: 485029. Face coverings are optional. The Court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law and Motion department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: Their name, CSR and telephone number, and their individual work email address. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. = (EPS/304)

rulings.Case Title: ALFONSO PAGANO JR ET AL VS. AKEBONO BRAKE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 1, 2025 in Department 304, Line 3: Defendant Nissan North America, Inc's., Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Opposition filed. Reply filed. A. Evidentiary Objections Defendant's objections in reply (Nos. 2 & 3) to the Bulletin are SUSTAINED under the Best (Secondary) Evidence Rule (Cal. Evid. Code 1520-23). All other objections are OVERRULED. B. Motion for Summary Adjudication Defendant brings this motion for summary adjudication only as to Plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages. The Third and Fourth causes of action have already been dismissed against Defendant. Plaintiff Alfonso Pagano Jr. ("Plaintiff") alleges that his mesothelioma was caused by exposure to asbestos products for which Defendant is liable. Specifically, Plaintiff worked at two tire and repair shops from 1985 to 1988, where he worked primarily on brakes and clutches. Plaintiff performed this work on Datsun and Nissan vehicles and alleges that Defendant is liable for the asbestos exposure he sustained in connection with those vehicles. (Opposition, 1.) *Complete tentative ruling has been electronically served to Counsel. The moving party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 304 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on April 1, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely via zoom: Meeting ID 160 757 8308; Passcode: 485029. Face coverings are optional. The Court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law and Motion department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: Their name, CSR and telephone number, and their individual work email address. There will be only one official record. rulings.Case Title: ALFONSO PAGANO JR ET AL VS. AKEBONO BRAKE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 1, 2025 in Department 304, Line 3. Defendant BMW of North America, LLC's Motion for Summary Judgment, or in the Alternative, Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Opposition filed. Reply filed. A. Judicial Notice Defendant's request for Judicial Notice of Plaintiffs' Complaint, Plaintiffs' Amended Complaint, and the pleadings, records, and files within the court file of this case is GRANTED, with the caveat that the Court is not judicially noticing the truth of the allegations therein. (Evid. Code, 452, subds. (c), (d).) B. Defendant's Evidentiary Objections Defendant's objections in reply are OVERRULED. C. Motion for Summary Judgment Defendant fails to sustain its initial burden of showing Plaintiff Alfonso Pagano Jr. ("Plaintiff") does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain needed evidence of asbestos exposure and causation. (Aguilar v. Atl. Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855.) Defendant relies on Plaintiff's deposition testimony and discovery responses arguing they are factually devoid. The Court disagrees. For the reasons set below, the Court finds that Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. D. Motion for Summary Adjudication Defendant's summary adjudication as to all five causes of action for: Negligence; Strict Liability; Negligent Misrepresentation; Fraud by Nondisclosure; Loss of Consortium; and Plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages are DENIED. Defendant's notice of motion is defective in that it fails to identify the causes of action as to which it seeks summary adjudication, and its separate statement fails to comply with the Rules of Court governing summary adjudication motions. (See Cal. Rules of Court, rule 3.1350(b),(d).) Further, the evidentiary showing made by Defendant in its moving papers for its motion for summary adjudication is wholly insufficient to shift the burden of production to Plaintiffs. *Complete tentative ruling has been electronically served to Counsel. The moving party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 304 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on April 1, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely via zoom: Meeting ID 160 757 8308; Passcode: 485029. Face coverings are optional. The Court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law and Motion department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: Their name, CSR and telephone number, and their individual work email address. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. = (EPS/304)

rulings.Case Title: DEBRA DELEUR VS. GENERAL ELECTRIL COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Advancing The Current Trial Date With Preference; And Extending Discovery Cutoff
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 1, 2025 in Department 304, Line 4. Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. 36(a). No Opposition filed. 1. The trial date is July 21, 2025, at 11:15 a.m. in Department 206. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is July 30, 2025. 2. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is July 15, 2025. 3. Time to respond to written discovery not yet served is shortened to 20 days. 4. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. 5. The fact discovery cut-off date is July 3, 2025. 6. The expert discovery cut-off date is July 18, 2025. 7. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than May 1, 2025. 8. No later than April 15, 2025: a. Plaintiff must provide a statement to each Defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting Plaintiff's claims as to each Defendant. b. Defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of facts supporting Defendant's affirmative defenses. c. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary judgment motion. 9. A further case management conference is set for June 3, 2025, at 4:00 p.m. 10. Plaintiffs will send a settlement demand to Defendants no later than June 9, 2025. Response to the settlement demand is due June 23, 2025. The parties shall separately submit confidential settlement demands/offers. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Department503@sftc.org via electronic mail on June 16, 2025. 11. A mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, July 17, 2025, at 9:30 a.m. in Department 503. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is excused for good cause. A request to excuse a personal appearance must be submitted to Department503@sftc.org two weeks before the mandatory settlement conference. *Complete tentative ruling has been electronically served to Counsel. The moving party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 304 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on April 1, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely via zoom: Meeting ID 160 757 8308; Passcode: 485029. Face coverings are optional. The Court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law and Motion department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: Their name, CSR and telephone number, and their individual work email address. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. = (EPS/304)
